verses.text: God blesses Noah and his sons: Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth. The same words from Genesis 1 — humanity gets a second chance and the same calling.
Two changes from Eden's original design. Animals now fear humans — the partnership of chapter 1 is wounded. And meat is now permitted as food. The world is still good, but no longer as it was.
Two changes from Eden's original design. Animals now fear humans — the partnership of chapter 1 is wounded. And meat is now permitted as food. The world is still good, but no longer as it was.
But meat must not be eaten with its blood. The Hebrew connects life and blood. Even in eating, humans are reminded that life belongs to God.
Whoever sheds human blood, by humans his blood will be shed. The reason given is profound: every person is made in God's image. The image of God in others is the reason murder is so serious.
Whoever sheds human blood, by humans his blood will be shed. The reason given is profound: every person is made in God's image. The image of God in others is the reason murder is so serious.
Be fruitful, and multiply; bring forth abundantly in the earth, and multiply therein. The blessing is repeated to make sure it lands. Life keeps being commanded.
God establishes a covenant with Noah, his descendants, and every living creature. The promise: never again will all flesh be cut off by a flood. The earth itself receives this promise.
God establishes a covenant with Noah, his descendants, and every living creature. The promise: never again will all flesh be cut off by a flood. The earth itself receives this promise.
God establishes a covenant with Noah, his descendants, and every living creature. The promise: never again will all flesh be cut off by a flood. The earth itself receives this promise.
God establishes a covenant with Noah, his descendants, and every living creature. The promise: never again will all flesh be cut off by a flood. The earth itself receives this promise.
And the token of the covenant is the rainbow. The Hebrew word literally means a war bow — but here it is set in the clouds, pointing away from the earth. God lays down His weapon.
And the token of the covenant is the rainbow. The Hebrew word literally means a war bow — but here it is set in the clouds, pointing away from the earth. God lays down His weapon.
When clouds gather and the rainbow appears, God says, I will remember my covenant. He doesn't need a visual reminder. We do. The rainbow is for our hearts, not His.
When clouds gather and the rainbow appears, God says, I will remember my covenant. He doesn't need a visual reminder. We do. The rainbow is for our hearts, not His.
The covenant is for every living thing, for perpetual generations. Every creature is included — the promise is cosmic, not just human.
The covenant is for every living thing, for perpetual generations. Every creature is included — the promise is cosmic, not just human.
Shem, Ham, and Japheth leave the ark. From them the whole earth is overspread. The three sons become the three branches of humanity.
Shem, Ham, and Japheth leave the ark. From them the whole earth is overspread. The three sons become the three branches of humanity.
Noah, the righteous man of chapter 6, plants a vineyard and gets drunk. Even saved humanity is still capable of falling. The story isn't sparing its heroes.
Noah, the righteous man of chapter 6, plants a vineyard and gets drunk. Even saved humanity is still capable of falling. The story isn't sparing its heroes.

Ham sees his father's nakedness and tells his brothers. Shem and Japheth walk in backwards with a covering, refusing even to look. Some children honor their parents in their weakness; others expose them.

Ham sees his father's nakedness and tells his brothers. Shem and Japheth walk in backwards with a covering, refusing even to look. Some children honor their parents in their weakness; others expose them.

Noah wakes up and pronounces destinies. Canaan, Ham's son, is cursed — this points forward to the wickedness of the Canaanite nations Israel will later meet. Shem and Japheth are blessed; Shem's line will carry the promise toward Abraham.

Noah wakes up and pronounces destinies. Canaan, Ham's son, is cursed — this points forward to the wickedness of the Canaanite nations Israel will later meet. Shem and Japheth are blessed; Shem's line will carry the promise toward Abraham.
Noah lived 350 years after the flood and died at 950. The last of the long-lived patriarchs. The next chapter introduces the world he leaves behind.
